hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified by the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel dimensions across lithiation states, those in which the stabilizing element X has an atomic radius that closely matches the void space of the Si sublattice framework will demonstrate a quantifiable threshold effect: alloys where this geometric compatibility falls within a specific tolerance window (predicted to be ±0.3 Å between X atomic radius and mean channel void radius) will exhibit volumetric expansion below 50% during full lithiation, whereas alloys outside this window will revert to expansion rates approaching that of pure Si regardless of their channel stability scores, a relationship testable by systematic DFT-relaxed molecular dynamics simulations across a curated set of binary Si–X compounds spanning a range of X atomic radii.
